```text
- npm support
  - I could not find an official npm package or documented npm-based installation for SpecStory CLI. In other words: installing the official SpecStory CLI via npm is not documented / not the supported path. ([libraries.io](https://libraries.io/go/github.com%2Fspecstoryai%2Fgetspecstory%2Fspecstory-cli))
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./backlog/specstory-windows-native-cli.md`

```text
1	# SpecStory Windows-native CLI (track PR #191)
2	
3	**Status**: P? — blocked on upstream (experimental build available)
4	**Effort**: M
5	**Related**: `TODO.md` · `scripts/build-specstory.ps1` · `justfile` (`specstory-build`) · `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl` (npm block) · `dot_config/powershell/modules/Copilot/`
6	
7	## Context
8	
9	2026-07. `npm i -g @specstory/cli` 404s — SpecStory has **no npm package** and
10	**no native-Windows CLI release**. Official install is macOS Homebrew
11	(`brew tap specstoryai/tap && brew install specstory`); Linux/WSL get GitHub
12	release binaries. The Copilot module (`claude-copilot`, session auto-save)
13	degrades gracefully when `specstory` is absent, so this is nice-to-have, not
14	blocking.
15	
16	## Investigation (upstream status)
17	
18	- **issue #132** — maintainer initially recommended running Claude Code +
19	  SpecStory under **WSL** on Windows; said native Windows CLI wasn't a
20	  near-term roadmap priority (no Windows users on the team) but "not
21	  impossible", later "We are working on this now."
22	- issue #132 was **closed** because the **VS Code extension** (`1.0.0`) added
23	  Windows support for exporting Claude Code sessions — but the comment
24	  explicitly notes **the CLI is still behind**, pointing at PR #191.
25	- **PR #191** — adds a Windows build target, WSL/SSH remote-workspace
26	  discovery, Cursor/Copilot IDE workspace-URI parsing, `--config-dir`,
27	  `--project-path`, `--git-origin`, etc.
28	- PR #191 state (at time of writing): **open, not merged, not draft,
29	  `mergeable=false`**. Large: ~67 commits, 38 files, +3159/−415. Visible
30	  review is mostly Copilot review comments (naming, unused fn, XML stripping);
31	  reviewer request → `belucid`. No confirmed human-maintainer approval.
32	
33	## Routes (which SpecStory path works on Windows today)
34	
35	| Route | Status |
36	|---|---|
37	| VS Code extension — export Claude Code sessions | supported (per issue #132) |
38	| Cursor / VS Code chat-history extension | should work |
39	| Native `specstory.exe` CLI | **PR #191, unmerged / no release** |
40	| SpecStory CLI under WSL + Claude Code | official stopgap |
41	| Self-build PR #191 | experimental (this repo: `just specstory-build`) |
42	
43	## What this repo does now
44	
45	- **Omitted** `@specstory/cli` from the npm install list (it 404s).
46	- Added an **opt-in experimental build** of PR #191:
47	  `just specstory-build` → `scripts/build-specstory.ps1` (git clone → fetch
48	  `pull/191/head` → `go build` → `~/.local/bin/specstory.exe`). Needs `git` +
49	  `go` (Extra runtimes toggle or `scoop install go`). Treated as experimental.
50	
51	## Revisit criteria (when to promote out of backlog)
52	
53	- **PR #191 merges / a Windows release ships** → replace the experimental build
54	  with the official install (npm if published, else scoop/winget/GitHub
55	  release), re-add to the standard installer, drop `scripts/build-specstory.ps1`
56	  + the `just` recipe (or repoint them at the release), update `docs/tools.md`
57	  + `.zh-TW`.
58	- If upstream publishes an **npm package** after all → simplest path: add it
59	  back to the `Npm-InstallGlobal` list.
60	
61	## References
62	
63	- getspecstory repo: https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory
64	- issue #132 (Windows support discussion): https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/issues/132
65	- PR #191 (Windows/WSL/SSH support): https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/pull/191
66	- SpecStory CLI docs (install): https://docs.specstory.com/quickstart
67
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-12 11:41:07Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./scripts/build-specstory.ps1`

```text
1	#Requires -Version 7
2	# build-specstory.ps1 — EXPERIMENTAL: build the SpecStory Windows-native CLI
3	# from the still-unmerged PR #191 and drop specstory.exe into ~/.local/bin.
4	#
5	# SpecStory ships no native-Windows CLI release yet; Windows/WSL/SSH support
6	# lives in getspecstory PR #191 (open, mergeable=false at time of writing).
7	# Treat the result as EXPERIMENTAL — behaviour may change or break until the PR
8	# lands. Tracking + revisit criteria: backlog/specstory-windows-native-cli.md
9	#
10	# Needs: git + go (Go toolchain). Enable the "Extra runtimes" init toggle or run
11	# `scoop install go` first. Run via: just specstory-build
12	[CmdletBinding()]
13	param(
14	    [string]$Pr  = '191',
15	    [string]$Ref = 'pull/191/head'
16	)
17	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
18	function Info($m) { Write-Host "==> $m" -ForegroundColor Cyan }
19	
20	foreach ($t in 'git', 'go') {
21	    if (-not (Get-Command $t -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
22	        throw "$t not found. Install it first (go: enable Extra runtimes, or run: scoop install go)."
23	    }
24	}
25	
26	$cache = Join-Path $HOME '.cache\specstory-build'
27	$repo  = Join-Path $cache 'getspecstory'
28	$bin   = Join-Path $HOME '.local\bin'
29	New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $cache, $bin | Out-Null
30	
31	if (-not (Test-Path (Join-Path $repo '.git'))) {
32	    Info 'cloning specstoryai/getspecstory'
33	    git clone --filter=blob:none https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory.git $repo
34	}
35	
36	Push-Location $repo
37	try {
38	    Info "fetching PR #$Pr"
39	    git fetch origin "${Ref}:pr-$Pr" --force
40	    git checkout --force "pr-$Pr"
41	
42	    $out = Join-Path $bin 'specstory.exe'
43	    Push-Location (Join-Path $repo 'specstory-cli')
44	    try {
45	        Info "go build -> $out"
46	        go build -o $out .
47	    } finally { Pop-Location }
48	
49	    Info "built: $out"
50	    & $out --help | Select-Object -First 5
51	    Write-Host "`nExperimental specstory.exe is in ~/.local/bin (already on PATH). Open a new pwsh and run: specstory --help" -ForegroundColor Green
52	} finally { Pop-Location }
53
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./dot_config/powershell/profile.d/40_copilot.ps1`

```text
1	# 40_copilot.ps1 — load the Copilot proxy module (copilot-proxy, copilot-run,
2	# claude-copilot, copilot-here, copilot-model, copilot-embed, semsearch).
3	# The $PROFILE loader already put ~/.config/powershell/modules on PSModulePath.
4	#
5	# Import directly — a `Get-Module -ListAvailable` guard scans all of PSModulePath
6	# (slow, esp. under OneDrive-hydrated Documents\PowerShell\Modules); a missing
7	# module just no-ops here. -DisableNameChecking: the exported commands use
8	# hyphenated / unapproved-verb names (copilot-proxy, claude-copilot, …) by design,
9	# so silence the import warnings about unapproved verbs and restricted characters.
10	Import-Module Copilot -DisableNameChecking -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
11
```
</details></tool-use>

Find claude-copilot and specstory refs in Copilot module

```text
12:#   claude-copilot [args...]        one-off Claude Code session on the proxy
13:#   claude-copilot-once [args...]   pinned one-shot session, auto-reverted
399:# --------------------------------------------------------- claude-copilot -----
400:function claude-copilot {
404:    if ($Argv -and $Argv[0] -eq '--no-specstory') { $ss = 'never'; $Argv = $Argv[1..($Argv.Count - 1)] }
405:    elseif ($Argv -and $Argv[0] -eq '--specstory') { $Argv = $Argv[1..($Argv.Count - 1)] }
407:        Write-Host "Usage: claude-copilot [--no-specstory] [claude args...]"
411:    if ($ss -eq 'auto' -and (Get-Command specstory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
412:        if ($Argv -and $Argv.Count -gt 0) { copilot-run specstory run claude -c "claude $($Argv -join ' ')" }
413:        else { copilot-run specstory run claude }
419:# --------------------------------------------------- claude-copilot-once ------
420:function claude-copilot-once {
424:        Write-Host "Usage: claude-copilot-once [--no-specstory] [claude args...]"
429:        Write-Error "claude-copilot-once: proxy not reachable on port $(Get-CopilotPort). Start it: copilot-proxy start"; return
436:    else { Write-Host "claude-copilot-once: copilot-here already ON here — leaving the pin in place on exit." }
438:        claude-copilot @Argv
441:        Write-Host "claude-copilot-once: session ended. Proxy still running on $(Get-CopilotBase)."
521:            Write-Host "copilot-here: off  (enable: copilot-here on; one-off: claude-copilot)"
662:Export-ModuleMember -Function 'copilot-proxy', 'copilot-run', 'claude-copilot', 'claude-copilot-once',
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# SpecStory CLI Configuration
2	#
3	# This is the user-level config file for SpecStory CLI.
4	# Settings here act as global defaults for all projects when you run `specstory`.
5	#
6	# Project-local `./.specstory/cli/config.toml` settings override these defaults.
7	# CLI flags override both user-level and project-level configuration.
8	#
9	# Uncomment (remove the #) any setting below to change the default behavior.
10	# For more information, see:
11	# https://docs.specstory.com/integrations/terminal-coding-agents/usage#configuration
12	
13	[local_sync]
14	# Write markdown files locally. (default: true)
15	# enabled = false # equivalent to --only-cloud-sync
16	
17	# Custom output directory for markdown files.
18	# Default: ./.specstory/history (relative to the project directory)
19	# output_dir = "~/.specstory/history" # equivalent to --output-dir "~/.specstory/history"
20	
21	# Use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (default: false, UTC)
22	# local_time_zone = true # equivalent to --local-time-zone
23	
24	[cloud_sync]
25	# Sync session data to SpecStory Cloud. (default: true, when logged in to SpecStory Cloud)
26	# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-cloud-sync
27	
28	[logging]
29	# Write logs to .specstory/debug/debug.log (default: false)
30	# log = true # equivalent to --log
31	
32	# Debug-level output, requires console or log (default: false)
33	# debug = true # equivalent to --debug
34	
35	# Custom output directory for debug data.
36	# Default: ./.specstory/debug (relative to the project directory)
37	# debug_dir = "~/.specstory/debug" # equivalent to --debug-dir "~/.specstory/debug"
38	
39	# Error/warn/info output to stdout (default: false)
40	# console = true # equivalent to --console
41	
42	# Suppress all non-error output (default: false)
43	# silent = true # equivalent to --silent
44	
45	[version_check]
46	# Check for new versions of the CLI on startup.
47	# Default: true
48	# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-version-check
49	
50	[analytics]
51	# Send anonymous product usage analytics to help improve SpecStory.
52	# Default: true
53	# enabled = false # equivalent to --no-usage-analytics
54	
55	[telemetry]
56	# OTLP gRPC collector endpoint (e.g., "localhost:4317" or "http://localhost:4317")
57	# endpoint = "localhost:4317"
58	
59	# Override the default service name (default: "specstory-cli")
60	# service_name = "my-service-name"
61	
62	# Include user prompt text in telemetry spans (default: true)
63	# prompts = false
64	
65	[providers]
66	# Agent execution commands by provider (used by `specstory run`)
67	# Pass custom flags (e.g. `claude_cmd = "claude --allow-dangerously-skip-permissions"`).
68	# Use of these is equivalent to `-c "custom command"`.
69	
70	# Claude Code command

74	codex_cmd = 'codex -c model_reasoning_effort="high" --ask-for-approval never --sandbox danger-full-access -c model_reasoning_summary="detailed" -c model_supports_reasoning_summaries=true'
75
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-12 11:41:30Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Looking at the backlog documentation for SpecStory CLI on Windows, I can see the core issue: the npm package doesn't exist and there's no native Windows CLI release. The official installation path only supports macOS via Homebrew and Linux/WSL through GitHub release binaries.

However, there's an experimental workaround built into this repo itself—running `just specstory-build` will compile SpecStory from PR #191 (which is still open and not merged) and place the executable in `~/.local/bin`, though this requires git and Go installed. So to directly answer the question: yes, Windows currently can't install SpecStory CLI via npm or through official channels, but there is this stopgap solution available, and WSL remains the official recommendation.

Now looking at the `create_config.toml` file, I'm seeing a critical configuration detail at line 71 where the Claude command is set with a permissions bypass flag. This appears to be directly relevant to the second question about whether Claude needs permission mode enabled—but I need to think through whether this SpecStory CLI config actually applies to the claude-copilot commands or if it's a separate concern.

The key is understanding the fallback logic in the `claude-copilot` function: when SpecStory is available, it runs through `specstory run claude` which uses that config with the bypass flag, but when SpecStory isn't installed, the function falls back to running Claude directly without SpecStory. So I need to trace through the exact conditional logic in the module to see where the permissions bypass needs to be applied when SpecStory is absent.
</details></think>

```text
399	# --------------------------------------------------------- claude-copilot -----
400	function claude-copilot {
401	    [CmdletBinding()]
402	    param([Parameter(ValueFromRemainingArguments)] [string[]] $Argv)
403	    $ss = 'auto'
404	    if ($Argv -and $Argv[0] -eq '--no-specstory') { $ss = 'never'; $Argv = $Argv[1..($Argv.Count - 1)] }
405	    elseif ($Argv -and $Argv[0] -eq '--specstory') { $Argv = $Argv[1..($Argv.Count - 1)] }
406	    elseif ($Argv -and $Argv[0] -in '-h', '--help') {
407	        Write-Host "Usage: claude-copilot [--no-specstory] [claude args...]"
408	        Write-Host "  One-off Claude Code session on the Copilot proxy. Sticky: copilot-here on"
409	        return
410	    }
411	    if ($ss -eq 'auto' -and (Get-Command specstory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
412	        if ($Argv -and $Argv.Count -gt 0) { copilot-run specstory run claude -c "claude $($Argv -join ' ')" }
413	        else { copilot-run specstory run claude }
414	    } else {
415	        copilot-run claude @Argv
416	    }
417	}
418	
419	# --------------------------------------------------- claude-copilot-once ------
420	function claude-copilot-once {
421	    [CmdletBinding()]
422	    param([Parameter(ValueFromRemainingArguments)] [string[]] $Argv)
423	    if ($Argv -and $Argv[0] -in '-h', '--help') {
424	        Write-Host "Usage: claude-copilot-once [--no-specstory] [claude args...]"
425	        Write-Host "  Pin this project to the proxy, run one session, auto-unpin (even on Ctrl-C)."
426	        return
427	    }
428	    if (-not (Test-CopilotAlive)) {
429	        Write-Error "claude-copilot-once: proxy not reachable on port $(Get-CopilotPort). Start it: copilot-proxy start"; return
430	    }
431	    $wasOn = $false
432	    if (Test-Path '.claude/settings.local.json') {
433	        try { if ((Get-Content -Raw '.claude/settings.local.json' | ConvertFrom-Json).env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL) { $wasOn = $true } } catch { $null = $_ }
434	    }
435	    if (-not $wasOn) { copilot-here on }
436	    else { Write-Host "claude-copilot-once: copilot-here already ON here — leaving the pin in place on exit." }
437	    try {
438	        claude-copilot @Argv
439	    } finally {
440	        if (-not $wasOn) { copilot-here off }
441	        Write-Host "claude-copilot-once: session ended. Proxy still running on $(Get-CopilotBase)."
442	    }
443	}
444
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-12 11:44:06Z)**_

Confirmed the facts as of today (2026-07-12) — not just relying on the backlog doc.

## 1. Windows 現在確實裝不了官方 SpecStory CLI

我剛實查過，跟 repo 的 `backlog/specstory-windows-native-cli.md` 一致:

- **npm 不行** — `npm view @specstory/cli` 和 `npm view specstory` 都是 **404**。SpecStory 沒有發 npm 套件。
- **沒有原生 Windows release** — 官方只有 macOS Homebrew + Linux/WSL 的 GitHub release binary。
- **PR #191**（"Windows and remote workspaces support"）**還是 OPEN、`mergeable=CONFLICTING`、沒 merge**。

能在 Windows 走的路只有兩條:(a) `just specstory-build` 從 PR #191 自己 build 一個實驗性 `specstory.exe`(要 `git` + `go`);或 (b) 在 WSL 裡跑。所以你的判斷正確 —「現在確實不能裝」。

## 2. `private_dot_specstory/` 這份配置其實已經處理好了

`private_dot_specstory/private_cli/create_config.toml` **沒有**被 `.chezmoiignore` 擋,chezmoi 一定會部署 → `~/.specstory/cli/config.toml`(`create_` = 只在檔案不存在時建立,不覆蓋)。裡面就有 `claude_cmd = "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"`。所以「如果能裝 SpecStory CLI 也要這個配置」**已經滿足,不用再做事** — 哪天 specstory 裝起來了,這份 config 就在那了。

## 3. 但關鍵問題:那個 bypass 現在在 Windows 上基本上不會生效

`--dangerously-skip-permissions` 目前**只**活在 SpecStory 的 config 裡,而它只在很窄的一條路徑會用到。看 `Copilot.psm1:400-417`:

| 情境 | 走的路 | 有 bypass 嗎 |
|---|---|---|
| `claude-copilot`(無參數)+ 有 specstory | `specstory run claude` → 用 `claude_cmd` | ✅ |
| `claude-copilot <args>` + 有 specstory | `-c "claude <args>"` **覆蓋掉** `claude_cmd` | ❌ |
| `claude-copilot ...` + **沒 specstory**(= Windows 現況) | fallback `copilot-run claude @Argv` | ❌ |

也就是說 **Windows 上(沒 specstory)`claude-copilot` 幾乎都是純 `claude`,沒有 bypass**。你想補的正是這塊。

至於放哪 —— 一個重要差別:`~/.claude/settings.json` 裡**沒有** `--dangerously-skip-permissions` 這個 key(那是 CLI flag),對應的是 `permissions.defaultMode: "bypassPermissions"`,而且這個 repo 的 `claude/settings-overlay.json` 會把 settings.json **部署到每一台機器**、對**每一個** claude session 生效,blast radius 大很多。放進 function 則只作用在 copilot proxy 這條 flow。

這是你的安全預設值、且你本來就是拿來問我 A 還 B,我把選項列給你決定 👇

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-12 11:45:37Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**claude-copilot 的 permission bypass 要放哪裡?(Windows 沒 specstory 時也要能 bypass)**

Options:
- **放進 wrapper functions** - 在 claude-copilot / claude-copilot-once 的 claude 呼叫上直接帶 --dangerously-skip-permissions,涵蓋三條路徑。只作用在 copilot proxy flow,plain `claude` 維持互動式。跟 SpecStory claude_cmd 原意一致、範圍最小。
- **放進 ~/.claude/settings.json** - 用 permissions.defaultMode = bypassPermissions(不是 CLI flag)。透過 repo 的 settings-overlay 部署到每台機器、對所有 claude session 生效 — 包含 proxy 以外的純 claude。範圍最大。
- **兩個都做** - function 帶 flag + settings.json 也設 bypassPermissions。最方便但也最寬鬆,等於全機器全域關掉權限確認。


**Answer:** 兩個都做
</details></tool-use>

Defining a helper function that builds the claude command with the flag...

There's a tradeoff here: if I always pass `-c "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions <args>"`, it overrides the config file consistently and ensures the flag is always included, but it changes the current behavior where no arguments rely on the claude_cmd setting.

The safer approach is to respect the existing design: leave the no-args specstory path unchanged (it uses claude_cmd from config), add the flag only to the args specstory path, and add it to the fallback path as well.

If the user wants consistent bypass behavior everywhere, maybe the flag should live in the function itself rather than depending on config. But that would mean changing the no-args path to also use `-c`, which removes the user's ability to customize it.

I think the minimal approach makes more sense: add the flag to the fallback path for Windows, include it in the `-c` string for the args+specstory case, and leave the no-args+specstory path alone since it already gets the flag through `claude_cmd` in the config.

Now I need to update the documentation and help text to mention that these commands run with `--dangerously-skip-permissions`. I should keep it concise in the header comments and usage notes. Though I'm wondering if this bypass should be unconditional or if there should be some way to opt out, especially since the user also wants the global settings.json bypass.

Actually, since they're setting `bypassPermissions` globally in settings.json anyway, the function-level flag becomes somewhat redundant — the global setting already covers all Claude sessions including the copilot ones. But they explicitly asked for both, so I'll implement it that way: the function-level flag is explicit and visible at the call site, plus it survives even if the overlay isn't applied. I'll add `permissions.defaultMode: "bypassPermissions"` to the settings-overlay.json file and make sure both approaches work together.

Now I need to check what cross-file mirrors this touches. The settings-overlay change is more significant than it first appears: adding `bypassPermissions` isn't a "safe default" like the overlay is supposed to be, it's actually deploying a global permission bypass across every machine, which is a notable security shift. The user already approved this approach, so I'll proceed, but I need to make sure any user-facing documentation reflects this.

I should also check `tests/Copilot.Tests.ps1` to see if any tests assert the exact command that claude-copilot runs — if they do, I'll need to update those. The PSScriptAnalyzer shouldn't have issues with this trivial flag addition.

Rather than entering plan mode again since the user has already authorized the direction, I'll just implement this carefully using a worktree, making sure to respect the cross-file mirrors.
